product_updates.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
product_updates.content: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.

product_updates.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
product_updates.content: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

product_updates.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
product_updates.content: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

product_updates.title: The “people’s airline” and the enterprise AI gold rush
product_updates.content: Everyone wants a piece of the enterprise AI pie, and this week, we saw a string of companies making their moves. product_updates.title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
product_updates.content: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

product_updates.title: The fax machine is the bottleneck in US healthcare, and VCs are starting to notice
product_updates.content: Like many AI companies automating work that humans currently do, Basata will eventually face a harder question about where the line is between augmenting workers and displacing them. For now, the founders say the administrative staff they work with aren't worried about that; they're more worried …

technical_breakthroughs.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
technical_breakthroughs.content: In the second week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk’s motivations for bringing the suit were under scrutiny. technical_breakthroughs.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
technical_breakthroughs.content: Across the country, schools and colleges postpone year-end tests.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech

technical_breakthroughs.title: Here’s how technology transformed babymaking
technical_breakthroughs.content: Technology is changing the way we make babies. The pioneering work of the scientists who invented IVF led to the birth of the first “test tube baby” in 1978.
